intent: Build parse_csv(text): the first line is a comma-separated header row; each following non-empty line is a data row of comma-separated values. Return a list of dicts, one per data row, mapping each header name to the corresponding value (values stay as strings). Assume no quoting or escaping. parse_csv('a,b\n1,2') returns [{'a': '1', 'b': '2'}].
oracle_test:
from csv_parse import parse_csv


def test_oracle():
    assert parse_csv('a,b\n1,2\n3,4') == [{'a': '1', 'b': '2'}, {'a': '3', 'b': '4'}]
    assert parse_csv('name\nAda') == [{'name': 'Ada'}]
    assert parse_csv('x,y,z\n1,2,3') == [{'x': '1', 'y': '2', 'z': '3'}]
